title: ScalarSummary payload and identity laws
description:
## Execution Mode
AFK — autonomous implementation after all listed blockers are closed.

## OpenSpec Scope
Change: `add-tray-capabilities`.
Requirement IDs: REQ-4, REQ-7, REQ-33.
Exact OpenSpec files: `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/proposal.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/design.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/tasks.md`, and `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/specs/payload-statistics/spec.md`.

## Design
Define `ScalarSchema` capturing numeric type and optional higher-moment flag (m3, m4). Identity is bound to this schema: `identity(schema)` returns a zero-valued ScalarSummary for the schema.

## End-to-End Behavior
Define and implement the ScalarSummary payload struct with required fields: `count`, `sum`, `sumsq`, `minimum`, `maximum`; and optional higher moments `m3`, `m4`. Implement `combine(x, y)` as component-wise aggregation: sum/sumsq/m3/m4 add, count adds, minimum takes the lesser, maximum takes the greater — in deterministic left-to-right order per REQ-3. Implement `identity(schema)` returning zero-valued scalars with the sentinel extrema defined in REQ-4: count=0, sum=sumsq=0, min=+Inf, max=-Inf, optional higher moments zero. Validate both identity laws at construction time. Construction rejects mismatched schema.

## Likely Files / Systems
`src/Tray.jl` and/or new `src/scalar_summary.jl`; `test/` focused tests; `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/specs/payload-statistics/spec.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- ScalarSummary construction produces correct identity results (both left and right identity laws hold).
- Identity has count=0, sum=sumsq=0, min=+Inf, max=-Inf, optional higher moments zero.
- `combine` correctly propagates sum, sumsq, count, min, max, and optional m3/m4.
- `combine` follows deterministic left-to-right order per REQ-3.
- Non-finite values outside the canonical sentinel extrema (min=+Inf, max=-Inf for identity) are rejected.
- Reversed extrema (min > max) or impossible sums are rejected.
- Schema mismatch raises an error.
- Edge case: single-leaf tree with identity payload — root fold equals identity.
- The focused tests for REQ-4, REQ-7, REQ-33 pass.
- `just test`, `ah check --changes add-tray-capabilities`, `openspec validate add-tray-capabilities --strict`, and `git diff --check` all exit 0.

## Focused Tests and Verification
Write focused unit tests for ScalarSummary construction, combine, identity laws, sumsq propagation, sentinel extrema validation, schema validation, and edge cases (identity leaf, b=2, n=1) under `test/`; then run `just test`, `ah check --changes add-tray-capabilities`, `openspec validate add-tray-capabilities --strict`, and `git diff --check`.

## Blockers
No prerequisites; this is the first ticket in the POC.

CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows. Write tests before implementation; clean up related code before adding functionality.
close_reason: ScalarSummary payload + identity laws implemented and tested. 13 test items pass covering: identity construction, identity laws, combine propagation, min/max, higher moments, schema mismatch, non-finite rejection, negative count, reversed extrema, associativity, edge values, identity leaf combination.

title: POC: Scalar aggregation tree with ScalarSummary
description:
## Goal

Build a working Proof-of-Concept of the core Tray abstraction: a balanced n-ary aggregation tree over ScalarSummary (sum, count, sum of squares, min, max) with canonical queries and point updates.

## Scope

- ScalarSummary payload with combine/identity and law validation
- Balanced n-ary tree construction with immutable leaf IDs
- Canonical range decomposition and target-depth queries
- Sequential atomic point updates with snapshot isolation
- Property tests (tree fold ≈ direct fold, log-depth bound)

## Out of scope

- AlignedArrayPayload, SamplePayload, sketches
- Multidimensional rollups and independent axes
- Concurrency, shared memory, dashboard
- Cross-process consistency (REQ-23, REQ-35, REQ-40)
- Structural mutations (insertion, removal — REQ-14, REQ-15, REQ-18)
- Attribution payload or compiler IR incrementalization
- Optional financial adapter

## Epic children

| Ticket | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| TRAYS-lep.1 | ScalarSummary struct, combine, identity, law tests |
| TRAYS-ogt | Generic balanced Tray construction |
| TRAYS-a0n | Canonical range and depth queries |
| TRAYS-ck3 | Sequential atomic point updates with snapshot isolation |
| TRAYS-lep.2 | Tree invariant and complexity property tests |

## Dependency chain

Tickets 1 (ScalarSummary) and 2 (tree construction) are parallelizable — payload algebra and tree skeleton are independent modules. Tickets 3→5 chain sequentially on ticket 2.

  lep.1 ─┬─→ ogt ─→ a0n ─→ ck3 ─→ lep.2
          └──→ (parallelizable)

## Success criteria

- `just test` passes with focused tests for REQ-1–4, REQ-7, REQ-9–13, REQ-31, REQ-33, REQ-34, REQ-42
- Tree root fold equals direct leaf fold for all deterministic fixtures
- Query results are deterministic across identical construction and update sequences
- All O(log_b n) depth/documentation claims are instrument-driven verified

title: Atomic point updates and snapshot isolation
description:
## Execution Mode
AFK — autonomous implementation after all listed blockers are closed.

## OpenSpec Scope
Change: `add-tray-capabilities`.
Requirement IDs: REQ-9, REQ-11.
Note: REQ-23, REQ-35, REQ-40 (cross-process consistency, concurrent readers, multi-node reads) are deferred to the full implementation and are NOT part of this POC ticket.

Exact OpenSpec files: `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/proposal.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/design.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/tasks.md`, and `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/specs/aggregation-tree/spec.md`.

## End-to-End Behavior
Apply point updates atomically in a single-threaded context so that readers observe either the complete old snapshot or complete new snapshot, never a torn state. When a leaf value is updated, every ancestor through the root is recomputed and published atomically. Injected update failure leaves all observable state unchanged.

## Likely Files / Systems
`src/Tray.jl`; anticipated focused `src/` modules for this capability; `test/` focused and integration suites; `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/proposal.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/design.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/tasks.md`, and `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/specs/aggregation-tree/spec.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- After a leaf update, only the affected leaf and its ancestors are recomputed; nodes outside the ancestor path remain unchanged.
- A point update either completely applies (leaf + all ancestors) or completely fails on injected error; no torn state is observable.
- The number of visited nodes for a point update grows as O(log_b n) for fixed branching factor b.
- Invalid update indices (outside 1:n) fail with a bounds error and leave every node unchanged.
- The focused tests for REQ-9, REQ-11 pass.
- `just test`, `ah check --changes add-tray-capabilities`, `openspec validate add-tray-capabilities --strict`, and `git diff --check` all exit 0.

title: Generic balanced Tray construction
description:
## Execution Mode
AFK — autonomous implementation after all listed blockers are closed.

## OpenSpec Scope
Change: `add-tray-capabilities`.
Requirement IDs: REQ-1–3, REQ-31, REQ-42.

## Design
Tolerance configuration and floating-point rebuild triggers (REQ-3) are deferred to post-POC. The POC uses exact equality for payload comparison.

## End-to-End Behavior
Construct a generic balanced Tray from valid inputs, preserving specified shape, payload identity, and invariants through the public API while rejecting invalid construction. Supports arbitrary payload type T with lawful combine/identity. Minimum branching factor b=2, maximum leaf depth O(log_b n), immutable leaf IDs, deterministic order.

## Likely Files / Systems
`src/Tray.jl`; anticipated focused `src/` modules for this capability; `test/` focused and integration suites; `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/proposal.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/design.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/tasks.md`, and `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/specs/aggregation-tree/spec.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- For every valid focused fixture the public constructor returns a balanced Tray with the specified root/leaf coverage and invariants, and every specified invalid fixture throws the documented error without a partial Tray.
- Edge cases: b=2 (minimum branching factor, deepest tree), n=1 (single leaf, depth=0, root=leaf), single leaf carrying identity payload.
- Edge cases: b > n (branching factor larger than leaf count produces a single-level tree with root covering all leaves).
- Invalid inputs: empty leaf array, b < 2 each raise a domain error.

title: Complete property-testing pilot documentation and acceptance
description:
## Parent

## Execution Mode

## What to build

Consolidate and complete contributor guidance added by the preceding slices for Supposition syntax, invariant-preserving generators, equality domains, deterministic budgets, shrinking/reproduction, and counterexample promotion. Document placement prohibitions, CI/coverage execution, Testaruda non-discovery, disabled example database, and full `Pkg.test()` as the sole supported pilot command. Preserve references to `TRAYS-719`, `TRAYS-ltz`, and `TRAYS-msh`.

Measure full-suite runtime and coverage against prior randomized loops, run the Rule of 5 implementation review, fix findings, and complete the OpenSpec checklist only where evidence exists. Report Espectacular findings without claiming durable mappings before `TRAYS-msh`.

**Files / Systems:**

- `openspec/changes/add-property-based-testing/tasks.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Randomized loops are removed only where stronger equivalent stable coverage is demonstrated.

title: Verify persistent-update equivalence and isolation
description:
## Parent

Epic: TRAYS-nyc

## OpenSpec traceability

- `openspec/changes/add-property-based-testing/design.md`: third independent-oracle property
- `openspec/changes/add-property-based-testing/tasks.md`: 2.1, 2.4, and 2.5
- `openspec/changes/add-property-based-testing/specs/property-based-testing/spec.md`: “Persistent generated update” scenario

## Execution Mode

AFK — autonomous implementation after `TRAYS-nyc.1`.

## What to build

Generate bounded compatible trees, valid leaf indices, and replacements by construction. Independently copy and replace the leaf vector and snapshot original levels. Prove the persistently updated tree has the same observable `b`, `schema`, and `levels` as a rebuild, and prove the original levels still equal the pre-update snapshot. Do not rely on undefined structural `Tree == Tree` behavior. Promote useful shrunk failures to ordinary regressions.

**Files / Systems:**
- ordinary property file under `test/`
- tree construction and persistent-update public APIs
- focused update tests in `test/runtests.jl`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Dimensions, schema, leaves, index, and replacement are compatible by construction without rejection-heavy filtering.
- Updated and independently rebuilt trees have equal `b`, `schema`, and `levels`.
- Original levels equal the pre-update snapshot after every update.
- The oracle does not depend on structural equality for `Tree`.
- Checks use stable names, explicit seeds, `db=false`, at most 100 examples, `n ≤ 32`, and `b ≤ 8`; numeric payloads are generated from bounded exactly representable observations under their generated schema.
- Useful shrunk failures become ordinary regressions and `just test` passes the full `Pkg.test()` suite.

## Blocked by

- `TRAYS-nyc.1` — verified Supposition integration boundary.

---
**CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows.**
- Write tests before writing implementation code.
- Clean up related code before adding new functionality.
acceptance_criteria: Generated persistent updates equal an independent rebuild in b/schema/levels, preserve the original levels snapshot, and pass Pkg.test().

title: Establish the Supposition integration boundary
description:
## Parent

Epic: TRAYS-nyc

## OpenSpec traceability

- `openspec/changes/add-property-based-testing/design.md`: “Use Supposition with verified baseline 0.3.5” and “Isolate Supposition behind an ordinary Test testset”
- `openspec/changes/add-property-based-testing/tasks.md`: 1.1–1.3 and relevant parts of 3.2–3.3
- `openspec/changes/add-property-based-testing/specs/property-based-testing/spec.md`: “Test-only property framework” and “Safe test-runner boundary”

## Execution Mode

AFK — autonomous implementation.

## What to build

Add Supposition 0.3.5 with compatibility below 0.4 strictly to the test environment. Include a dedicated property file from an ordinary top-level `Test.@testset` in `test/runtests.jl`; add a named smoke property with explicit RNG, `db=false`, and bounded examples. During TDD, prove a deliberately failing variant shrinks and makes `Pkg.test()` fail, then retain only the passing variant.

Document why checks cannot be nested in `@testitem`, run bare at top level, or use `record=false`/private report fields. Document full `Pkg.test()` (invoked by `just test`) as the supported pilot invocation and Testaruda's inability to select individual checks. Warn that a future move into ReTestItems-scanned worker files must preserve a separate ordinary-Test path or first verify supported custom-testset interoperability.

**Files / Systems:**
- `Project.toml`, `Manifest.toml`
- `test/runtests.jl`
- dedicated property file under `test/` without `_test.jl` or `_tests.jl` suffix
- `docs/src/dev/testing.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Supposition resolves from baseline 0.3.5 with compatibility below 0.4 under extras/test targets only.
- Loading Tray outside the test target neither imports nor requires Supposition.
- If the manifest resolves a later compatible 0.3.x patch, the same passing/failing integration gate succeeds before acceptance.
- The passing smoke property and existing ReTestItems test items both execute under full `Pkg.test()`.
- A temporary failing variant is observed to shrink and produce nonzero package-test status, then removed.
- Documentation records supported/prohibited placement, deterministic configuration, the future-runner migration warning, full-suite command, and selection limitation.
- `just test` runs full `Pkg.test()` successfully with only the retained smoke property.

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately.

---
**CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows.**
- Write tests before writing implementation code.
- Clean up related code before adding new functionality.
acceptance_criteria: Supposition is test-only; an ordinary-Test smoke property gates Pkg.test(); deliberate failure propagation and shrinking are verified; runner limitations are documented.

description:

Close all child tickets, truthfully complete the OpenSpec checklist, pass strict validation, and keep Supposition test-only.

title: Resolve ScalarSummary extrema change semantics
description: Property-based testing investigation found that change_between(old::ScalarSummary, new) claims arbitrary transformation, while apply_change only widens extrema with min/max and cannot represent narrowing such as [1,10] to [2,9]. Decide whether changes are append-only or replacement-capable, align the contract and implementation, and add the unconstrained round-trip law once resolved.
acceptance_criteria: ScalarSummary change semantics are explicitly specified; implementation and tests agree; arbitrary round-trip is either satisfied or clearly excluded by the public contract.

title: Propose Supposition property-based testing adoption
description: Document and specify a safe Supposition.jl adoption path for Tray.jl, including the ReTestItems integration boundary, pilot properties, CI/tooling effects, generator constraints, replay policy, and the ScalarSummary finite-change contract issue discovered during investigation.
acceptance_criteria: A strictly valid OpenSpec change contains proposal, design, tasks, and test-strategy delta; documents verified compatibility behavior and keeps implementation gated on approval.
close_reason: Created and strictly validated add-property-based-testing OpenSpec proposal with design, tasks, requirements, and follow-up issues.
